findings.title: Command injection via unchecked PRD/spec content in VERIFICATION.md generation
findings.suggested_fix: Change the heredoc from << EOF to << 'EOF' so variable and command substitution are disabled, or write the file via a here-document with escaped content using printf.
evidence: Line 213 extracts ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA from PRD.md using grep/sed without sanitization.
Line 227 extracts DECISION_DESC from spec.md using awk without sanitization.
Line 239 uses cat << EOF (unquoted heredoc), which performs both variable expansion and command substitution on $ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA and $DECISION_IMPLEMENTATION.
If PRD.md or spec.md contain lines like '- [ ] AC-001: $(rm -rf /) test', the shell executes rm -rf / during heredoc expansion.

findings.title: CHECK-2 exit gate hard-fails in non-interactive environments due to read prompts
findings.suggested_fix: Add a --non-interactive / --ci flag (or environment variable overrides like COMPILE_CONFIRM=yes) so the script can run in CI pipelines without stdin. Alternatively, attempt automatic compilation instead of prompting.
evidence: Line 57: read -r -p '代码是否已编译通过? (yes/no): ' COMPILE_CONFIRM 2>/dev/null || COMPILE_CONFIRM='no'.
When stdin is closed (e.g., CI, ssh, piped execution), read returns non-zero and COMPILE_CONFIRM defaults to 'no'.
Line 61-62: COMPILE_CONFIRM='no' sets EXIT_CODE=1 and the script exits 1 at line 198 before generating VERIFICATION.md.
Line 111: similar read prompt for missing compliance report also defaults to 'no' and sets EXIT_CODE=1, making the gate unusable in automation.

findings.title: Compliance report location mismatch between worktree and project directory
findings.suggested_fix: Search for compliance-review-report.md in both PROJECT_DIR and WORKTREE_DIR, or explicitly document that the report must be copied/symlinked to PROJECT_DIR before running the exit gate.
evidence: skills/tech-code/SKILL.md Phase 3.1 says coding happens in a superpowers-created worktree.
agents/compliance-reviewer.md runs during Phase 4 (in the worktree context) and writes compliance-review-report.md.
Line 68: COMPLIANCE_FILE='$PROJECT_DIR/compliance-review-report.md' only looks in the project directory, ignoring WORKTREE_DIR.
If the compliance report is generated inside the worktree and not manually copied back, check-gate-2-exit.sh warns 'compliance-review-report.md 不存在' and defaults to failing in non-interactive mode.

findings.title: Stale compliance report from previous run can bypass CHECK-2 exit gate
findings.suggested_fix: Validate the compliance report age (e.g., check mtime is newer than the latest code change) or embed a git commit hash / checksum in the report and verify it matches the current worktree.
evidence: Line 68-86: If compliance-review-report.md exists in PROJECT_DIR, the script parses BLOCK/WARN counts without checking whether the report corresponds to the current code changes.
A stale report from a previous feature (or an earlier iteration of the current feature) with total_block: 0 would cause the gate to pass even if the current code has compliance issues.
The front-matter parsing at line 82-85 is particularly fragile: a user can prepend 'total_block: 0' to any report to force a pass.

findings.title: pattern-scan.sh silently loses data when Java filenames contain spaces
findings.suggested_fix: Quote all variable expansions passed to grep/wc/xargs, or refactor to use find -print0 | xargs -0 grep.
evidence: Line 47: controllers=$(find ... -name '*Controller.java' ...).
Line 65: grep -h '^@RestController|^@Controller' $controllers 2>/dev/null — $controllers is unquoted, causing word splitting on spaces.
If a file is named 'My Controller.java', grep receives two paths: 'path/to/My' and 'Controller.java', neither of which exists, so annotations for that file are silently omitted.
Same issue affects scan_services (line ~567), scan_repositories (line ~605), scan_entities (line ~3678), scan_configs (line ~3743), and scan_exceptions (line ~3783 uses xargs without -0).

findings.title: Test race condition on shared fixture directory leaves stale SPEC-STATE.md
findings.suggested_fix: Create a temporary copy of the sample-feature fixture in TEST_BASE_DIR, write SPEC-STATE.md there, run the gate, and clean up the temporary copy.
evidence: Line 305: cat > '$pass_dir/SPEC-STATE.md' writes to $PROJECT_ROOT/tests/fixtures/sample-feature/SPEC-STATE.md.
Line 344: rm -f '$pass_dir/SPEC-STATE.md' cleans up only if the test reaches that point.
If the test is interrupted (Ctrl+C, timeout, signal), the cleanup does not run and SPEC-STATE.md remains in the git-tracked fixture directory.
Concurrent test runs on the same machine would race on this shared file, potentially causing flaky test results.

residual_risks: check-gate-2-exit.sh parses compliance reports with regex heuristics rather than a strict schema validator; future format changes could silently break gate behavior.
pattern-scan.sh has no timeout on find/grep operations; running against a large monorepo could block the code phase indefinitely.
testing_gaps: No test exercises check-gate-2-exit.sh with a non-interactive stdin (CI scenario).
No test verifies pattern-scan.sh behavior when source filenames contain spaces or special characters.
No test validates that check-gate-2-exit.sh rejects a stale or manually crafted compliance-review-report.md.
